mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances despite the declining unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization improvements and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in >60% of post-shock recovery observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.5% MoM are followed by positive 20-day forward returns in >65% of instances, refuting the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate creates a virtuous labor market feedback loop that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to generate a statistically significant positive Rank IC (>0.04) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts during late-cycle expansion phases signals deteriorating consumer balance sheets and reduced credit demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing start declines >5% MoM in late-cycle regimes are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of cases.
